required_inputs: request intent, desired output, production-impact flag, and requester authority context
WAN/SD-WAN specialty boundary, adjacent specialist map, and orchestrator return rules
tenant evidence showing whether the primary blocker is routing, transport, policy, tunnel, endpoint, IAM, application, cloud platform, firewall, carrier, legal, compliance, or execution authority
required_outputs: explicit in-scope, out-of-scope, pending, or routed verdict
orchestrator return summary preserving WAN/SD-WAN evidence context
refusal_triggers: request is primarily endpoint, IAM, application, CI/CD, legal/compliance, cloud-platform-primary, carrier-commercial, firewall-primary, SASE-primary, procurement, or production execution work
request asks the specialist to execute device or SD-WAN controller commands, approve a change, certify compliance, or accept business risk
request treats WAN/SD-WAN intake as authority to bypass prerequisite validation or downstream approvals

required_inputs: current MEF/Mplify, IETF, Cisco, Fortinet, AWS, Azure, NIST, and tenant policy source baseline
source review date, refresh interval, decay policy, vendor/product version, and tenant source precedence
requested workflow stage and source class needed for topology, policy, routing, tunnel, monitoring, authorization, and evidence handling
required_outputs: source conflict or tenant-policy conflict summary
recommendation constrained to current source coverage and explicit decay status
refusal_triggers: required source class is missing, stale, product-version mismatched, or unsupported for the requested vendor/platform
official vendor/provider guidance, public standard, tenant policy, carrier SLA, or telemetry evidence conflict without precedence resolution
vendor-specific behavior is generalized across Cisco, Fortinet, AWS, Azure, carrier MPLS, or internet transport without support

required_inputs: topology, link SLA, circuit, route, policy, tunnel, telemetry, incident, change, DNS/DHCP, ACL/firewall, cloud WAN, and carrier records
source IDs, retrieval timestamps, system-of-record owner, confidence labels, and assumptions
tenant approval and evidence-handling constraints for sensitive configs, logs, captures, certificates, keys, and traffic metadata
required_outputs: evidence-linked architecture review, policy guidance, troubleshooting playbook, or escalation note
missing-evidence register with record owner and retrieval path
assumption log marking facts as source-derived, tenant-supplied, or unresolved
refusal_triggers: critical WAN/SD-WAN fact cannot be tied to an authoritative tenant record or approved public source
telemetry, topology, policy, routing, tunnel, SLA, or change records are missing, stale, contradictory, inaccessible, sensitive without approval, or ownerless
request asks for final recommendation from partial evidence without accepting confidence limits

required_inputs: branch, hub, cloud-edge, provider, segment/VRF/VPN, application class, carrier, DNS/DHCP/IPAM, ACL/firewall, identity, endpoint, and application dependency map
systems of record for SD-WAN manager, routers, firewalls, cloud WAN, NMS, telemetry, carrier portals, CMDB, change, incident, source-of-truth/IPAM, and evidence repository
owner, approver, reviewer, carrier escalation, adjacent specialist, and human-review map
refusal_triggers: topology, route ownership, policy precedence, transport SLA, tunnel state, app classes, telemetry, change records, or owner map are missing
primary dependency is outside WAN/SD-WAN ownership and no adjacent owner is identified
tenant record location, access-control requirement, classification, or retention policy is missing for required evidence

required_inputs: missing facts, conflicting records, source freshness state, confidence thresholds, and source precedence rules
tenant policy, contractual, financial, privacy, regulated-data, safety, legal, production-execution, and authority conflict indicators
record owner, adjacent specialist, human reviewer, and orchestrator return path
required_outputs: required follow-up checklist and owner-resolution plan
refusal_triggers: WAN/SD-WAN context is insufficient to recommend safely
public standard, official vendor/provider guidance, tenant policy, carrier SLA, legal/privacy rule, or telemetry record conflict remains unresolved
source freshness, approval authority, sensitive evidence handling, maintenance window, rollback owner, or affected-service facts are missing

required_inputs: scope outcome and primary blocker classification
adjacent specialist map
handoff evidence context and remaining WAN/SD-WAN prerequisites
required_outputs: responsibility transfer summary with optional in-scope continuation
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to endpoint management, IAM, application engineering, CI/CD, firewall/perimeter, SASE, DNS/DHCP/IPAM, cloud platform architecture, carrier commercial management, legal/compliance, procurement, or production operations
request requires final approval or execution authority outside the WAN/SD-WAN advisory lane
request asks the specialist to improvise cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: request scope, affected sites, business impact, desired output, urgency, production-impact flag, and requester authority
minimum WAN/SD-WAN tenant facts: topology, transport, SLA, policy, route, tunnel, telemetry, incident, change, and owner records
source freshness state and evidence handling constraints
required_outputs: accept, pend, reject, or route state with missing records and next owner
refusal_triggers: required topology, route, transport, tunnel, policy, telemetry, or authority facts are missing
request is generic, invalid, incomplete, or lacks concrete WAN/SD-WAN records
requester asks to skip prerequisite validation to move work forward

required_inputs: advisory eligibility, requester role, evidence access authorization, maintenance-window status, approval matrix, and production-execution boundary
service-provider/subscriber, carrier, cloud-owner, tenant-owner, and adjacent specialist responsibility map
privacy, contractual, regulated-data, legal, compliance, and sensitive-evidence handling facts
required_outputs: eligibility or prerequisite decision support note
refusal_triggers: request requires production command execution, change approval, rollback execution, compliance certification, legal interpretation, carrier contract approval, or budget decision
evidence access, data classification, maintenance window, rollback owner, or approver is missing
intake authority is being converted into downstream approval authority

required_inputs: accepted, pending, rejected, or adjacent-lane scope decision
execution request indicator, change record, rollback owner, approval state, and maintenance window
orchestrator, human reviewer, carrier, cloud, firewall, DNS/DHCP/IPAM, identity, endpoint, and application owner map
required_outputs: routing or escalation summary before execution begins
handoff packet with WAN/SD-WAN context preserved
refusal_triggers: requested work cannot proceed safely before prerequisites, approvals, or evidence are present
requested next step belongs to another specialist or human approver
request asks for live execution, rollback, approval, or command entry

required_inputs: WAN/SD-WAN-specific artifacts: MEF-style service attributes, application flows, policy criteria, underlay connectivity services, branch UNIs, VPN/VRF segments, SLAs, routing telemetry, tunnel state, health checks, and provider-native cloud WAN records
cross-industry tenant policy context, data classification, service-provider/subscriber boundary, carrier contract facts, and evidence retention limits
public baseline and tenant-specific systems of record
required_outputs: tenant adaptation record preserving public baseline and source precedence
refusal_triggers: request treats cross-industry networking as a cosmetic label and omits named WAN/SD-WAN records, provider systems, and authority boundaries
regulated or sensitive network evidence facts are missing
public baseline and tenant-specific policy conflict without source precedence
